message:
perf(vdbe): promote IfPos into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Fifth opcode-promotion lever on bd-1dp9.6.2, following AddImm
(64d8ce4d), Copy (20b05a95), SCopy (bd1a49bc), and DecrJumpZero
(7b7bd71c). `Opcode::IfPos` is the canonical OFFSET counter (25
codegen sites): fires once per skipped row in any LIMIT/OFFSET-bounded
query. Body is a single integer compare + optional `set_reg_int` +
unconditional pc reassignment — mirror-structure to DecrJumpZero,
small enough for dispatch-shortening to clear the noise floor.

Mirrors the existing main-match arm verbatim. Adds a
`test_if_pos_via_hot_path` unit test pinning the three-way state
machine (val>0 taken / val==0 fall-through / val<0 fall-through) to
the hot-path arm. The pipeline_stages bench harness for IfPos was
landed earlier in c63bd6f5/7dc9ed35.

Win sizes (criterion, p<0.05 unless noted, /256 signal-cleanest size):

| variant | before | after | Δ thrpt |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| /64   | 981.24 ns | 799.76 ns | +18.7% (p=0.00) |
| /256  | 3.6100 µs | 2.8947 µs | +19.6% (p=0.00) |
| /1024 | 18.778 µs | 11.503 µs | +33.3% (p=0.00; baseline noisy) |

Larger than the projected ~5% (mirror of DecrJumpZero's +5%) because
the IfPos main-match arm sat in a particularly cold position in the
jump table. Re-ran the four prior-promotion benches twice as an A/B
safety check; deltas stayed within ±2% (noise edge), with the second
sample showing AddImm and Copy each unchanged or marginally better
and DecrJumpZero +1.9% — small relative to IfPos's -19.6% on every
OFFSET row.

Bench: `cargo bench -p fsqlite-vdbe --bench pipeline_stages -- \
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_ifpos --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 4`

`test_if_pos_via_hot_path` passes alongside the existing
`test_if_not_zero_decrements_and_jumps`.

Bead: bd-1dp9.6.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
